basic_details.job_title: Punjab Revenue Department Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online for 09 Manager, DEO and More Posts
basic_details.short_information: Government of Punjab Department of Revenue Rehabilitation and Disaster Management has released the official NAKSHA Scheme SPMU Notification 2026 on the official website https://revenue.punjab.gov.in/. A total of 09 vacancies have been announced under the Punjab Revenue Department Recruitment 2026. Eligible candidates aged between up to 35 to 70 years (post-wise) with qualifications ranging from Bachelor’s Degree to Master’s Degree in relevant fields along with required experience can apply online from ongoing to 15 May 2026. The official notification PDF and online application link are availab

application_fee.all categories: NIL (No application fee mentioned in the official notification)

age_limit.project coordinator: 45 years (as on last date of application)
age_limit.details: The upper age limit for each post under the Punjab NAKSHA Scheme SPMU Recruitment 2026 varies based on the nature and seniority of the role. Age will be calculated as on the last date of submission of application i.e. 15/05/2026. The post-wise age limit is given in the table below.

qualification.project coordinator: Education: Master's in Engineering / Business Administration / Development Studies / Social Work OR Bachelor's in Engineering / Science or related field. Work Experience: 1) Minimum 5 years' experience in implementing and coordinating projects related to Central/State Government programmes or public/private sector schemes. 2) Proven track record in data analysis, project management and coordination, demonstrating effective leadership and problem-solving skills in achieving project objectives and ensuring smooth programme operations.
qualification.expert (land administration): Work Experience: Retired Civil Service Officers with a minimum of 10 years of experience in land administration, revenue/registration/consolidation/survey.
qualification.manager (geospatial technology): Education: Bachelor's degree in Engineering/Technology from a recognized University or Institute in a relevant field such as Civil Engineering, Computer Science, Geomatics Engineering, or related disciplines. GATE qualified and certification in GIS/Data analysis related courses with knowledge of ArcGIS/QGIS and other software related to GIS data processing and management is desirable. Work Experience: At least 5 years of experience in managing and execution of GIS-related projects. Proficiency in utilising GIS software such as ArcGIS, QGIS and other relevant tools for spatial data processing, mapping and database management.
qualification.programmer (mis & gis developer): Education: Bachelor's degree in Engineering or Science, preferably in Computer Science or Information Technology. Work Experience: Minimum of 5 years' experience in database administration, encompassing database design, implementation, optimisation and maintenance. Proficiency in GIS-based software tool development, with a track record of designing and deploying solutions to meet project requirements.
qualification.gis technical expert: Education: Bachelor's degree in Engineering or Master's in Computer Applications/Geo-informatics or relevant fields. Work Experience: A minimum of 5 years of experience in the development of GIS-based software tools. Demonstrated proficiency in designing, developing and implementing GIS solutions to meet project requirements and objectives.
qualification.remote sensing analyst: Education: Bachelor's degree in Engineering or Master's in Computer Applications/Geo-informatics or relevant fields. Work Experience: A minimum of 5 years of experience in maintaining High Resolution Satellite Imageries and software tools like ERDAS Imagine, LPS, QGIS, ArcGIS is preferred. Demonstrated proficiency in Ortho rectification, mosaicking, analysis using satellite data to meet project requirements and objectives.
qualification.data entry operator: Education: Bachelor's degree in any stream. Work Experience: Minimum of 2 years of experience and proficient typing skills in either English or Punjabi are preferred. Possession of valid certification in MS Word and MS Excel / MS Office issued by a recognised institute certified under ISO standards is desirable.
qualification.details: Before applying for the Punjab NAKSHA Scheme SPMU Recruitment 2026, candidates must ensure they meet all the prescribed eligibility conditions including educational qualification, work experience, and age limit for the respective post. Candidates not possessing the requisite qualifications are liable to be rejected. Candidates who have pursued professional courses through Open and Distance Learning (ODL) are not eligible to apply. The post-wise eligibility criteria are given below. | Nationality: Only Indian nationals are eligible to apply. ODL Restriction: Candidates who have pursued professional courses through Open and Distance Learning (ODL) are not eligible. Foreign Degree: Candidates possessing degrees from foreign universities must produce an equivalency certificate issued by the Association of Indian Universities (AIU), New Delhi at the time of interview or while applying. | Post-wise Educational Qualification & Work Experience

selection_process: Post Name Educational Qualification Marks Experience Marks Technical Certifications Interview Marks Total Marks
Project Coordinator 40 Marks (Bachelor's 30 + Master's 10 extra) 35 Marks – 25 Marks 100 Marks
Expert (Land Administration) 40 Marks (Bachelor's 30 + Master's 10 extra) 35 Marks – 25 Marks 100 Marks
Manager (Geospatial Technology) 40 Marks (Bachelor's 30 + Master's 10 extra) 30 Marks 5 Marks 25 Marks 100 Marks
Programmer (MIS & GIS Developer) 40 Marks (Bachelor's 30 + Master's 10 extra) 30 Marks 5 Marks 25 Marks 100 Marks
GIS Technical Expert 40 Marks (Bachelor's 30 + Master's 10 extra) 30 Marks 5 Marks 25 Marks 100 Marks
Remote Sensing Analyst 40 Marks (Bachelor's 30 + Master's 10 extra) 30 Marks 5 Marks 25 Marks 100 Marks
Data Entry Operator 50 Marks (Bachelor's 40 + Master's 10 extra) 50 Marks Typing Test (Mandatory – Qualifying) – 100 Marks
Minimum Typing Speed 30 Words Per Minute (WPM)

The selection process for Punjab NAKSHA Scheme SPMU Recruitment 2026 is based on a screening and interview mechanism. Candidates will be shortlisted based on their educational qualification marks and relevant experience marks as per the scoring criteria defined for each post.

Only the Top 50 shortlisted candidates (based on Sections A and B scores) will be called for interview/technical interaction. The final merit list will be prepared by adding screening marks and interview marks. The post-wise selection criteria with total marks of 100 are detailed below.
how_to_apply: The application process for Punjab NAKSHA Scheme SPMU Recruitment 2026 is strictly online. Applications must be submitted in the prescribed proforma through the official website https://revenue.punjab.gov.in/ on or before 15/05/2026. Applications received after the due date, unsigned applications, or incomplete applications (without photograph, signature, mark sheets, or incomplete in any respect) will not be considered. The step-by-step process to apply is given below.
Visit the official website https://revenue.punjab.gov.in/ and find the NAKSHA Scheme recruitment advertisement.
Download and fill the prescribed application proforma carefully with all required details.
Attach attested copies of all certificates, mark sheets, a recent passport-size photograph, and relevant experience certificates.
If applying for more than one post, submit a separate application for each post.

If Grades/CGPA are awarded instead of marks, candidates must indicate the equivalent percentage as per the University's conversion procedure and submit necessary proof. Rounding off of percentage of marks/CGPA grading is not allowed.
Submit the completed application online at https://revenue.punjab.gov.in/ before the closing date of 15/05/2026.
Keep a copy of the submitted application form for future reference.
For any queries, contact Under Secretary (Revenue) at [email protected].
important_instructions: Candidates are advised to read all instructions carefully before applying for the Punjab NAKSHA Scheme SPMU Recruitment 2026. Non-compliance with any of the instructions may result in rejection of the application at any stage of the recruitment process. The key instructions issued by the Department of Revenue, Rehabilitation and Disaster Management are listed below.

Applications must be submitted online in the prescribed proforma along with attested copies of all certificates, mark sheets, a recent passport-size photograph, and relevant experience certificates. Candidates not possessing the requisite qualifications for the posts are liable to be rejected. Candidates who have pursued professional courses through Open and Distance Learning (ODL) are not eligible to apply. Only shortlisted candidates will be called for interview. Candidates called for interview must produce all relevant original documents along with a set of self-attested photocopies at the time of the selection process. No TA/DA shall be paid to candidates for appearing in the interview. Candidates are advised to check their email and visit the Revenue Department's website from time to time. The Department will not be responsible for non-receipt of intimation regarding call letters or any communication due to technical reasons or otherwise. Only Indian nationals need to apply. Candidates intending to apply for more than one post must submit a separate application for each post. Applications received after the due date, unsigned applications, or incomplete applications will not be considered. The Department reserves the right to accept or reject any application without assigning any reason thereof. Canvassing in any form will lead to summary disqualification. If any information furnished in the application form is found to be wrong, false, or incomplete, the candidate will not be called for interview. Candidates who fail to produce original degree or diploma certificates, proof of date of birth, conversion factor of CGPA, mark sheets, etc., will not be entertained.

faq.question: Q1. What is Punjab Revenue Department NAKSHA Scheme SPMU Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Punjab Revenue Department NAKSHA Scheme SPMU Recruitment 2026 is a contractual hiring drive by the Department of Revenue, Rehabilitation and Disaster Management, Government of Punjab, to fill 09 specialist posts in the State Programme Management Unit for the NAKSHA land records modernisation project.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are released under Punjab NAKSHA Scheme Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: A total of 09 posts have been notified under the NAKSHA Scheme SPMU, including positions such as Project Coordinator, Expert (Land Administration), Manager (Geospatial Technology), Programmer (MIS & GIS Developer), GIS Technical Expert, Remote Sensing Analyst and Data Entry Operator.

faq.question: Q3. What is the last date to apply online for Punjab Revenue Department Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the online application form for Punjab Revenue Department NAKSHA Scheme SPMU Recruitment 2026 is 15 May 2026, and applications must be submitted through the jamabandi.punjab.gov.in recruitment portal.

faq.question: Q4. What are the salary details for NAKSHA Scheme SPMU posts in Punjab?
faq.answer: The monthly remuneration ranges from ₹45,000 for the Data Entry Operator post to ₹1,50,000 for the Project Coordinator and Expert (Land Administration) posts, with other technical roles like Manager (Geospatial Technology), Programmer and GIS Expert drawing between ₹80,000 and ₹1,20,000 per month.

faq.question: Q5. What is the nature and tenure of Punjab Revenue Department NAKSHA Scheme jobs?
faq.answer: All positions are purely contractual under the NAKSHA Scheme, with an initial tenure of one year in the State Programme Management Unit, extendable based on project requirements and performance as per government norms.

faq.question: Q6. Who can apply for the Expert (Land Administration) post under Punjab NAKSHA Scheme?
faq.answer: Only retired Civil Service Officers with at least 10 years of experience in land administration, revenue, registration, consolidation or survey work are eligible to apply for the Expert (Land Administration) post.

faq.question: Q7. What is the age limit for Punjab Revenue Department Recruitment 2026 posts?
faq.answer: The upper age limit varies by post: 45 years for Project Coordinator, 70 years for Expert (Land Administration) and 35 years for Manager (Geospatial Technology), Programmer, GIS Technical Expert, Remote Sensing Analyst and Data Entry Operator, calculated as on 15 May 2026.
